activities_projects.title: Imagining a Magical Object
steps: Think about a magical object you would like to have.
Describe what powers it would have and how it would work.
Write a short paragraph explaining how you would use it.
Share your idea with classmates.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance imagination and descriptive writing skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Character Analysis – Gopi
activities_projects.objective: Understand the character traits of Gopi
steps: Read the poem carefully.
List the important actions of Gopi in the story.
Identify at least three character traits of Gopi such as kindness, courage, or honesty.
Support each trait with examples from the poem.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how to analyze characters using textual evidence.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of traits and supporting examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Theme and Moral Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand the moral and themes of the poem
steps: Discuss in groups the main message of the poem.
Explain how Gopi uses the magic brush differently from the Zamindar.
Write a short paragraph about the moral of the story.
Share your conclusions with the class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify themes such as kindness, generosity, and justice.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of understanding and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sequencing the Story
steps: Read the poem carefully.
Identify the key events in the story.
Arrange the events in chronological order.
Explain how each event leads to the next.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve reading comprehension and sequencing skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct order and explanation of events (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Symbolism in the Poem
steps: Identify important symbols such as the magic brush, the fortress, and the river.
Discuss what each symbol represents in the story.
Write a short explanation for each symbol.
Present your interpretations to the class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how objects in literature represent deeper ideas.
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of interpretation and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play – Gopi and the Zamindar
steps: Form groups and assign roles such as Gopi, the Zamindar, and villagers.
Prepare a short dialogue based on the poem.
Recreate the scene where Gopi refuses to paint treasures.
Perform the role play in front of the class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve confidence, expression, and understanding of the story.
activities_projects.evaluation: Expression, teamwork, and understanding of roles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Tone and Mood Identification
activities_projects.objective: Understand how tone changes in the poem
steps: Identify parts of the poem that show different moods.
Classify them as playful, suspenseful, or celebratory.
Write examples from the poem that support your answer.
Discuss how the tone changes as the story progresses.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how tone influences storytelling.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification with supporting examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary and Expression Activity
activities_projects.objective: Improve vocabulary and understanding of expressions
steps: Read the vocabulary words and expressions from the poem.
Match them with their meanings.
Create your own sentences using these words.
Share the sentences with the class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve vocabulary usage and sentence formation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct usage and clarity of sentences (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Writing – If I Had a Magic Brush
steps: Imagine you had a magic brush like Gopi.
Write a short story about what you would paint.
Explain how your drawings would help people.
Share your story with the teacher or class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance creative thinking and narrative writing.

activities_projects.title: Design Your Own Magical Object
steps: Design a magical object that can help people.
Draw the object and label its special features.
Write a short description of its powers.
Present your creation to the class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop imagination, design thinking, and presentation skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and clarity of explanation (10 marks)
